_meta.source_codes: ADMIN ONLY — NCT=NCERT, RDS=RD Sharma style, RSA=RS Aggarwal style, EXM=NCERT Exemplar, CBP=CBSE Past Paper, OLY=Olympiad pattern, ORI=Original

questions.question_text: How many independent measurements are needed to construct a unique quadrilateral?

solution_steps.working: A quadrilateral has 4 sides + 4 angles = 8 measurements. But knowing 5 independent ones uniquely determines the quadrilateral.
solution_steps.voice_text: To construct a unique quadrilateral, we need exactly 5 independent measurements — sides, angles, or diagonals.

questions.question_text: To construct a parallelogram, what is the minimum number of measurements needed? Name them.
questions.correct_answer: 3: two adjacent sides and one angle (or one diagonal)

solution_steps.working: Opposite sides equal, opposite angles equal. So if we know 2 adjacent sides and 1 included angle, all else is determined.
solution_steps.voice_text: Because a parallelogram has special properties, we only need 3 measurements — two adjacent sides and one angle between them.

questions.question_text: Describe the steps to construct a quadrilateral ABCD where AB = 5 cm, BC = 4 cm, CD = 3 cm, DA = 4.5 cm and diagonal AC = 6 cm.
questions.diagram_description: Quadrilateral ABCD constructed using diagonal AC=6cm as base. Triangle ABC drawn first (AB=5,BC=4,AC=6), then triangle ACD (AC=6,CD=3,DA=4.5).
questions.correct_answer: Construction using diagonal to split into two triangles

solution_steps.working: Draw a base line AB of length 5 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: Start with the base side AB of 5 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Locate C using BC and AC
solution_steps.working: With B as centre, draw arc of radius 4 cm. With A as centre, draw arc of radius 6 cm. Intersection is point C.
solution_steps.voice_text: Draw arcs from B with radius 4 and from A with radius 6. Where they meet is point C.

solution_steps.instruction: Locate D using CD and DA
solution_steps.working: With C as centre, draw arc of radius 3 cm. With A as centre, draw arc of radius 4.5 cm. Intersection is D.
solution_steps.voice_text: Similarly locate D using CD = 3 cm and DA = 4.5 cm.

solution_steps.working: Join A-B-C-D-A to complete the quadrilateral.
solution_steps.voice_text: Join all four vertices in order to complete the quadrilateral.

questions.question_text: Describe the steps to construct a square with side 4.5 cm.
questions.diagram_description: Square ABCD with all sides 4.5 cm. All angles 90 degrees. Constructed with set square or compass.
questions.correct_answer: Construction using perpendicular lines at each vertex

solution_steps.working: Draw base AB.
solution_steps.voice_text: Draw the base AB of 4.5 cm.

solution_steps.working: Using compass or set square, construct angle of 90° at B. Mark BC = 4.5 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: At B, draw a perpendicular line and mark C at 4.5 cm.

solution_steps.working: Construct 90° at A. Mark AD = 4.5 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: Similarly draw the perpendicular at A and mark D.

solution_steps.working: DC should equal 4.5 cm. Join to complete the square.
solution_steps.voice_text: Join D to C. Verify DC equals 4.5 cm. The square is complete.

questions.question_text: Describe steps to construct a rhombus PQRS with side 5 cm and one diagonal PR = 8 cm.
questions.diagram_description: Rhombus PQRS. Diagonal PR = 8 cm drawn first. Q and S located using arcs of radius 5 cm from P and R. Both lie equidistant from PR.
questions.correct_answer: Construction using diagonal and equal sides

solution_steps.working: Draw PR of length 8 cm as base.
solution_steps.voice_text: Start with the diagonal PR of 8 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Locate Q using arcs from P and R
solution_steps.working: With P as centre, arc radius 5 cm. With R as centre, arc radius 5 cm. They meet at Q.
solution_steps.voice_text: Since all sides of a rhombus are equal, draw arcs of 5 cm from both P and R. They meet at Q.

solution_steps.instruction: Locate S on the other side
solution_steps.working: Repeat: arcs of 5 cm from P and R on the other side. They meet at S.
solution_steps.voice_text: S is the mirror image of Q on the other side of the diagonal.

solution_steps.working: Join P-Q-R-S-P.
solution_steps.voice_text: Join all vertices. The rhombus is complete. Verify all sides are 5 cm.

questions.question_text: When constructing a quadrilateral with 4 sides and 1 diagonal given, into how many triangles is it divided?

solution_steps.working: One diagonal splits the quadrilateral into 2 triangles.
solution_steps.voice_text: A diagonal divides a quadrilateral into exactly 2 triangles, which we construct separately.

questions.question_text: Describe the steps to construct a rectangle ABCD where AB = 6 cm and BC = 4 cm.
questions.diagram_description: Rectangle ABCD. AB=CD=6cm, BC=AD=4cm. All angles 90 degrees.

solution_steps.working: Draw base AB.
solution_steps.voice_text: Start by drawing AB of 6 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Construct 90° at A, mark AD = 4 cm
solution_steps.working: Perpendicular at A. Mark D at 4 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: Construct a right angle at A and mark D at 4 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Construct 90° at B, mark BC = 4 cm
solution_steps.working: Perpendicular at B. Mark C at 4 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: Similarly construct the right angle at B and mark C.

solution_steps.working: DC should = 6 cm. Verify with ruler.
solution_steps.voice_text: Join D to C. Measure DC — it should be 6 cm. If correct, the rectangle is complete.

questions.question_text: Construct a quadrilateral ABCD with AB = 4 cm, BC = 5 cm, angle B = 60°, angle C = 120° and CD = 3 cm.
questions.diagram_description: Quadrilateral ABCD. AB=4cm at base. Angle B=60 degrees, BC=5cm. Angle C=120 degrees, CD=3cm. D connected to A.

solution_steps.working: Base AB of 4 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: Draw the base AB of 4 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Construct angle B = 60°, mark BC = 5 cm
solution_steps.working: At B, using protractor draw 60°. Mark C at 5 cm on this ray.
solution_steps.voice_text: At B, construct an angle of 60 degrees and mark C at 5 cm.

solution_steps.instruction: Construct angle C = 120°, mark CD = 3 cm
solution_steps.working: At C, measure 120° from CB. Mark D at 3 cm.
solution_steps.voice_text: At C, construct 120 degrees from the direction of CB and mark D at 3 cm.

solution_steps.working: Join D to A to complete the quadrilateral.
solution_steps.voice_text: Join D to A. The quadrilateral is complete.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum data required to construct a unique rhombus? Give one example set of data.
questions.correct_answer: One side and one angle (or one side and one diagonal)

solution_steps.working: All sides equal in a rhombus. So side length is just one measurement. One angle (or diagonal) determines the shape.
solution_steps.result: 2 measurements: side + angle or side + diagonal
solution_steps.voice_text: A rhombus has all sides equal. So we only need the side length and one angle — or side and one diagonal — to construct it uniquely.

questions.question_text: A quadrilateral can be divided into ____ triangles by drawing one diagonal.

solution_steps.working: One diagonal joins two opposite vertices, splitting the quadrilateral into 2 triangles.
solution_steps.voice_text: One diagonal divides a quadrilateral into 2 triangles.
